name: bytheslice
description: ByTheSlice is a Claude Code plugin that takes a project from a free-form brief to a shipping production web app through a phased, subagent-driven workflow. v3 collapses ship-feature + ship-frontend into a single /bytheslice:deliver-stage loop that routes by stage type, dispatches the right sub-skill (init-design-system / scaffold-ci-cd / setup-environment) automatically, and gates every slice on a real verification (lint/type/build + a type-aware aggregating test review including dev-server boot and Claude-in-Chrome browser UAT for frontend slices). The design-system stage scaffolds an operator-only /library preview route with copy-Markdown-link source-path buttons on every page H1 and state H3; frontend stages then pass through a non-skippable Library Preview Gate (Phase 4.5) — Phase 0 extend-vs-create check, self-critique block, clickable preview URLs, then HARD STOP for explicit user approval before any production-route import or consumer-side edit lands. /deliver-stage stops at 'slice committed locally, ready for review'; /bytheslice:ship-pr is the universal closeout — push, PR (carrying the closing-narrative paragraph forward via the commit body), CI watch with auto-fix loop on red (capped at 3 attempts), user-authorized merge, main sync, branch + worktree cleanup. Run /deliver-stage then /ship-pr per slice; /run-pipeline is the experimental autonomous multi-stage variant.
